concerns.text: FR-001 mandates the system to 'halt with a [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] flag' if all sources fail. The spec contains a literal '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]' marker in the requirements and user stories (US-1, FR-001). Per the kickback reason, a converged spec must not carry this marker; the behavior must be explicitly defined (e.g., 'halt with a specific error code and log message') rather than deferring the definition to a placeholder.

User Story 1, Acceptance Scenario 3 repeats the '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]' marker. This is a placeholder for a requirement that was not resolved. The spec must define the exact system behavior (e.g., exit code, log format) for the 'missing variable' case instead of using a placeholder marker.
